drills: Shadow batting — practice strokes without a ball to groove technique
Throw-down sessions — face rapid throws from 16-18 yards
Bobble feed drill — react to unpredictable bounce off a bobble surface
Short ball drill — practice pull and hook shots with tennis balls
tips: Keep your head still and eyes level at point of delivery
Lead with your front elbow for straight drives
Practice weight transfer — back foot for short, front foot for full length
Work on leaving deliveries outside off stump

drills: Target bowling — place cones on good length spots and aim consistently
Run-up rhythm drill — mark your run-up and practice smooth acceleration
Slower ball variations — practice knuckle ball, back-of-hand, and off-cutter
Bowling with a scrambled seam to improve wrist position awareness
tips: Maintain a high arm action for pace and bounce
Focus on seam position rather than just speed
Use the crease — bowl from wide, close, and around the wicket
Build a stock ball first, then add variations

drills: Catching — high catches, slip catching, close-in reflex catches
Ground fielding — attack the ball, pick up and throw in one motion
Relay throws — practice long-distance accuracy from the boundary
Diving practice — use crash mats to build confidence in diving catches
tips: Always be on the balls of your feet, ready to move
Watch the ball into your hands — soft hands for catches
Practice throwing from different angles and distances
Work on your underarm flick for run-out situations

injury_prevention: Warm up thoroughly before bowling — focus on shoulders and lower back
Strengthen your core to protect your lower back during fast bowling
Do regular shoulder mobility exercises to prevent rotator cuff injuries
Wear proper protective gear — pads, gloves, helmet, abdominal guard

drills: Shadow footwork — move to all 6 corners of the court without a shuttle
Ladder drills — improve foot speed and coordination
Split step practice — time your split step with opponent's shot
Multi-shuttle drill — rapid movement to retrieve shuttles placed around court
tips: Always return to base position after each shot
Use a split step before every shot to improve reaction time
Stay light on your feet — minimize contact time with the floor
Practice lunging depth to extend your reach without losing balance

drills: Wall rally — practice clears and drives against a wall
Smash practice — feed high shuttles and focus on contact point
Net kill drill — practice tight net shots and net kills from the front court
Cross-court drop shot practice for deception
tips: Use a relaxed grip and tighten only at the point of impact
Pronation is key for powerful smashes — rotate your forearm
Disguise your shots by using the same preparation for different strokes
Practice backhand from the rear court — it's a common weakness

injury_prevention: Strengthen wrist and forearm muscles to prevent tennis elbow
Stretch your calves and Achilles regularly — high injury risk in badminton
Wear proper court shoes with good lateral support
Do shoulder strengthening exercises — rotator cuff work is essential

drills: Form shooting — close-range one-handed shooting to perfect mechanics
Spot-up shooting — shoot from 5 spots around the arc
Catch-and-shoot — practice receiving a pass and shooting in rhythm
Free throw routine — shoot 50-100 free throws daily
tips: BEEF: Balance, Eyes on target, Elbow aligned, Follow through
Arc matters — aim for a 45-degree arc on your shot
Practice your release point — consistent release = consistent shots
Shoot game-speed shots in practice, not lazy reps

drills: Stationary dribbling — crossovers, between-the-legs, behind-the-back
Full-court dribbling — speed dribble with both hands
Cone dribbling — weave through cones at game speed
Tennis ball drill — dribble while catching a tennis ball with the other hand
tips: Keep your head up — never look at the ball while dribbling
Pound the ball hard — low dribble is harder to steal
Develop your weak hand until it's as comfortable as your strong hand
Use change of pace, not just direction, to beat defenders

injury_prevention: Strengthen ankles with resistance band exercises to prevent sprains
Wear properly fitted basketball shoes with ankle support
Land on both feet after jumping to reduce knee stress
Do ACL prevention exercises — hamstring curls, single-leg squats

drills: Approach and jump drill — practice the 3-step approach without the ball
Wall hitting — practice arm swing and contact point against a wall
Setter-hitter connection — work with a setter on timing and placement
Cross-court vs line hitting — practice aiming different angles
tips: Use a 3-step approach: left-right-left (for right-handers) or right-left-right
Contact the ball at the highest point of your jump
Snap your wrist on contact for topspin and power
Watch the setter's hands — start your approach when the ball is set

drills: Footwork drill — slide step along the net, jump and press
Read blocking — react to the setter's delivery and move to the hitter
Solo block repetitions — practice timing your jump to match the hitter
Double block coordination — practice with a partner
tips: Penetrate your hands over the net — seal the block
Keep your eyes on the hitter's arm, not the ball
Stay on the balls of your feet for quick lateral movement
Don't jump too early — time your jump to the hitter's approach

injury_prevention: Strengthen shoulders with internal and external rotation exercises
Do prehab for patellar tendon — squats, step-ups, eccentric exercises
Tape or brace fingers if you're a setter or blocker
Practice safe landing mechanics to prevent ankle injuries

drills: Sprint intervals — 10x 30m sprints with 60-second rest
Acceleration drill — 3-point stance to 10-yard burst
Agility ladder — fast feet patterns for quickness
Shuttle runs — 5-10-5 pro agility drill
tips: Drive your knees high and pump your arms for maximum speed
Lean forward during acceleration, upright at top speed
Work on your first 5 steps — they determine most plays
Strengthen your hip flexors for faster stride frequency

drills: Passing accuracy — hit targets at various distances
First touch control — receive passes from different angles
1v1 dribbling — beat a defender in a confined space
Crossing and finishing — deliver crosses and convert them
tips: Keep the ball close when dribbling in tight spaces
Use both feet — practice your weaker foot daily
Look up before passing — scan the field
Practice under pressure — never train at walking pace

injury_prevention: FIFA 11+ warm-up program reduces injuries by up to 50%
Strengthen hamstrings — Nordic hamstring curls are essential
Warm up for at least 15 minutes before any match or training
Wear shin guards — they prevent a surprising number of fractures

drills: Ball toss practice — consistent toss height and placement
Serve from the service line — focus on placement, not power
Shadow swing — practice the full service motion without a ball
Target practice — place cones in service box corners
tips: A reliable second serve is more important than a booming first serve
Use continental grip for serves — it enables spin
Toss the ball slightly in front and to the right (for right-handers)
Use your legs — power comes from the ground up, not just the arm

drills: Cross-court rally — 50 consecutive cross-court backhands/forehands
Feed and hit — have a partner feed balls to practice specific shots
Approach shot to volley — transition from baseline to net
Movement drill — practice recovery steps between shots
tips: Prepare early — turn your shoulders as soon as you read the ball
Hit through the ball — extend your arm toward the target
Use topspin for consistency and margin over the net
Stay balanced through your shots — don't fall away

injury_prevention: Stretch your wrist and forearm before and after playing
Use the right string tension — too tight increases arm stress
Strengthen your core to protect your back during serves
Wear court-specific shoes — running shoes cause ankle injuries on court

drills: Block starts — practice explosive starts from blocks
Flying 30m — build up speed then sprint at max effort for 30m
Resistance sprints — use a sled or parachute for strength
Plyometric bounds — develop explosive power for acceleration
tips: Drive phase (first 30m): low body angle, powerful arm drive
Upright phase: tall posture, relaxed shoulders, fast turnover
Practice relaxation — tension slows you down
Strengthen your glutes — they're the primary sprinting muscle

drills: Tempo runs — sustained effort at 70-80% of max heart rate
Interval training — 800m repeats with 2-minute rest
Long runs — weekly long run at conversational pace
Fartlek — mix speed bursts with easy jogging
tips: Build mileage gradually — no more than 10% increase per week
Easy days should feel genuinely easy — most runners go too fast
Race pace practice — include some work at your target race pace weekly
Sleep is your #1 recovery tool — aim for 8+ hours

injury_prevention: Don't skip rest days — overtraining is the #1 cause of injury in athletics
Strengthen small stabilizer muscles — ankles, hips, glutes
Replace running shoes every 500-700 km
Get a gait analysis to ensure your running form isn't causing issues
